description_long: This is a compact, all-black submachine gun with a minimalist, utilitarian design reminiscent of the British Sten gun. The rifle features a cylindrical metal receiver and barrel, a simple tubular folding stock, and a vertical pistol grip. The magazine is inserted from the side, a distinctive feature of certain historical submachine guns. The surface is matte and appears to be made primarily of steel, with some polymer or plastic components for the grip. The overall construction is robust, with minimal external embellishments, and the weapon is designed for ease of manufacture and reliability. The iron sights are integrated into the top of the receiver, and the muzzle is plain, lacking any visible suppressor or flash hider.
description: A compact, matte-black submachine gun with a tubular folding stock, side-mounted magazine, and minimalist design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length, folding stock, and vertical pistol grip.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the barrel opening and the circular end of the folding stock.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the side-mounted magazine and ejection port.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tubular stock and the back of the receiver.
